title: Thoughts about tattoo (4/21/23 fragment)
html:
TL;DR

  • One-paragraph fragment: getting a tattoo/piercing feels like "crossing a line into a realm of potential stain you won't come back from."
  • Cuts off mid-sentence — "the more realistic and..." — never finishes the counter-thought.
  • Surfaced in a private conversation with Ayden; same body-modification anxiety thread as piercing prompts.
  • Adds a 2023 datapoint to the body as permanent record tension that recurs across Yahir's ink/piercing notes.

Notes

The whole document is two sentences: anxious framing of tattoos as a one-way door, paired with the self-correction "that's probably the anxious bottled-up version." Truncated. Reads like a dictation he never returned to. Sits alongside piercing prompts in the small body-modification corner of the vault — same vocabulary of line-crossing and realm. Worth keeping for the metaphor: a tattoo as stain, not as decoration. That word choice maps to the narcissist mirror anxiety — being marked permanently is the same shape as being marked by lineage.

Connections

  • ayden · piercing prompts · body as permanent record
  • narcissist father as mirror · 2023 02 16 thoughts and things on 21523
